Age distribution of patients who are hMPV positive
Overall, there was a significant difference in HMPV prev-
alence between patients >5–14 years (0.7%, 5/734) and
those ≤5 years (2.2%, 98/4399) (P=0.004). The patients
were divided into six age groups: 0–3 months, >3–6
months, >6–12 months, >1–2 years, >2–5 years and >5–14
years. The distribution of HMPV prevalence between Figure 2 Seasonal distribution of human metapneumovirus
these age groups was significantly different (P=0.03), and infection in paediatric patients hospitalised with acute
